title: Domestic Consumption of Oil Products, Iran 1964-1969 (thousand metric tons)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank); underlying data from National Iranian Oil Company (via Bank Markazi Iran reports 1969-1970)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION. Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_cep_vol7_statistical_annex.pdf ('Current Economic Position and Prospects of Iran', Volume VII: Statistical Annex, May 18 1971, Report No. SA-23a), Table 8.8, page 84 of the PDF. Rendered to PNG via pdftoppm -r 200 and visually read. No value interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
units: thousand metric tons (converted from barrels by the source at 7.6 bbl=1 Mton for fuel oil/gas oil/kerosene/gasoline, ~7.0 bbl=1 Mton for other products)
notes: Breaks Iran's domestic oil-product consumption into fuel oil, gas oil, kerosene, gasoline, and other products (incl. liquid gas sold by National Iranian Gas Co.), each with 1964-69 and 1968-69 growth rates in the source (not reproduced here, only levels). Kerosene is of particular household-consumption interest (heating/cooking fuel for much of the non-electrified population this era) -- 1965's anomalous spike to 2,216 (vs, 1,188 in 1964 and 1,266 in 1966) is exactly as printed in the source, not a transcription artifact (double-checked against the rendered image). Companion/predecessor to wb1962-gasoline-consumption-1955-1962 (which only covered gasoline, 1955/56-1961/62, in million liters) -- together the two tables give a near-continuous 1955-1969 gasoline consumption picture across two different units (reconciliation left to data/processed/).
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription with visual verification, pdftoppm -r 200 render + Read tool)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
